title: I turned down $500K at FAANG to stay indie. No regrets.
post:
I had an offer on the table: $500K total comp at a FAANG company. Dream salary for most people. And I turned it down to keep building my own thing.

People thought I was crazy. Maybe I was. But here's what I've learned in the three years since: money without freedom is a bad trade. I was optimizing for the wrong thing. The corner office, the prestige, the number in my bank account — none of it mattered as much as I thought it would.

I'm not telling you to reject big offers. I'm telling you to know what you're actually optimizing for. Most people never stop to ask that question. They just chase the biggest number and wonder why they're miserable.

What would you turn down half a million dollars for? Thoughts?
